label: p10_wave1_blocked_on_t16_correctness_regression
hardware: local RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 24 GiB card; W7900 rerun unverified
commits: 731241a feat(P10.B1): wire Q4T16 selected dual WMMA prefill into compact MoE dispatch
bb0c933 feat(P10.B2,P10.B3): Q5_K T16 + Q6_K T16 selected WMMA prefill kernels
ed14ed5 feat(P10.B4): Q8_0 T16 dense WMMA prefill kernel + dispatch wire

fixtures.tests/test_gguf_k_t16_selected_wmma_pref: 22 passed (Q5T16 + Q6T16 selected WMMA prefill, BF16/FP16, multiple shapes)
fixtures.tests/test_gguf_q8_0_t16_wmma_prefill.py: 75 passed (Q8T16 dense WMMA prefill, BF16/FP16/F32 x BF16/FP16/F32 x rows {4,16,17,32,33,48,64} x shapes (64-512, 16-128) x all 6 allowed tile shapes)
fixtures.tests/test_qwen35_gguf_compact_moe_wmma_: 7 passed (compact MoE dispatch resolver routes to T16 quant keys correctly)

e2e_correctness.method: P9.E2 KL/top-1 gate plus public LLM.generate smoke
e2e_correctness.conclusion: T16 decode-repack path itself is broken at the model level; the issue predates P10 work. P9.G1 retained commit 7ffb4e9 also produces incoherent text when DECODE_REPACK=1 is set. The P9.E2 gate historically passed because the pre-prior-agent safety gate blanket-disabled WMMA prefill / GEMV decode for qwen35moe, so candidate effective_use_wmma=false / effective_use_gemv=false even with --use-wmma-prefill / --use-gemv-decode requested; both reference and candidate ran the pristine row-GEMV path. The 506 tok/s number was kernel-throughput-correct but model-output-broken.

prefill_512x0_benchmark.command: HIPENGINE_GGUF_DECODE_REPACK=1 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/qwen35_gguf_bench.py --model /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf --quant gguf_q4_k_m --prompt-length 512 --decode-tokens 0 --warmup-runs 1 --measured-runs 3 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --use-gemv-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build

prefill_512x128_benchmark.command: HIPENGINE_GGUF_DECODE_REPACK=1 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/qwen35_gguf_bench.py --model /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf --quant gguf_q4_k_m --prompt-length 512 --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-runs 1 --measured-runs 3 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --use-gemv-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build

rocprof_512x0.command: HIPENGINE_GGUF_DECODE_REPACK=1 HIPENGINE_COMPILER_VERSION_FILE=/tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt rocprofv3 --kernel-trace -o p10b1234_512_0 --output-format csv -- python3 scripts/qwen35_gguf_bench.py --model /models/gguf/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-UD-Q4_K_M.gguf --quant gguf_q4_k_m --prompt-length 512 --decode-tokens 0 --warmup-runs 0 --measured-runs 1 --force-bulk-prefill --bulk-prefill-attention-mode bulk --use-wmma-prefill --use-gemv-decode --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build

remaining_selected_t16_gemv_decode_slive.note: residual decode-shape calls at prefill rows; small and bounded

hipengine_pre_p10_p9_g1.correctness: kernel_correct_model_broken (same pre-P10 T16 issue, but hidden by safety gate disabling effective fastpaths)

acceptance.blocking_issue: P10.X1 pre-P10 T16 decode-repack model correctness regression
acceptance.blocking_reason: Cannot promote a benchmark row that produces incoherent text on the smallest LLM.generate fixture, even though the kernels are throughput-correct.

decision: Record as diagnostic-only. Do NOT promote to benchmarks/README.md or benchmarks/CHANGELOG.md until P10.X1 lands. Wave 1 kernel work is sound; the upstream T16 decode-repack path needs a separate correctness fix. See docs/GGUF.md P10.X1 section and WORKLOG.md entry 2026-05-20 for the bisection trace and proposed next steps.
notes: Bench script is shape-driven and checks finite logits, not text coherence. Both pre-P10 (506/98) and post-Wave-1 (1900/98) measurements are kernel-throughput-correct.
All P10 Wave 1 kernels pass kernel-level CPU-oracle correctness (rtol=2e-2, atol=5e-1) per AGENTS.md gate.
Target hardware is W7900 per docs/PLAN.md; this row was measured on the local RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 24 GiB card. W7900 rerun is part of the formal acceptance once P10.X1 lands.
